summary: Source-linked financial data layer for analysts, models, APIs, MCP, and AI agents.
editor_note: The sharp bet is that financial AI fails at the spreadsheet cell, not the chat box. Daloopa covers 5,500+ public companies with source-linked numbers, Excel add-ins, Scout, API, MCP, cloud delivery, and an agent benchmark. Unlike generic market-data feeds, it is selling audit trails for every answer. The open question is whether analysts trust agents when a filing source is one click away.

product_evidence.claim: The official site positions Daloopa as a source-linked financial data layer with Data Sheets, Excel add-ins, Scout, API, MCP, and cloud integrations.
The MCP page says Daloopa covers 5,500+ public-company tickers, exposes 5-10x more data points, and preserves document attribution.
The API product page presents Daloopa data as a programmatic feed for financial models, research workflows, and downstream AI systems.

market_context.primary_user: Investment analysts, financial-data teams, and builders of source-grounded finance agents.
market_context.differentiation: Filing-linked audit trails and analyst workflow integrations instead of a generic market-data or chat interface.
market_context.why_now: The May 28, 2026 Series C led by Brighton Park Capital funds the trusted data layer Daloopa is pitching for financial AI agents.

risks.claim: The risk is whether finance teams will let agents act on source-linked data, or keep the product as analyst-side evidence infrastructure.
risks.basis: VentureDex editorial assessment based on official product evidence and funding-source review.
